Container.Name: OpenThreadBorderRouter-HA
Container.Overview:
[b][u]OpenThread Border Router (Home Assistant optimized) for Unraid[/u][/b]
Defaults are configured for [b]LAN / network-based RCP[/b], but this template supports both LAN and USB radios.
[b]Mode selection:[/b] - [b]LAN mode (default):[/b] set [i]NETWORK_DEVICE[/i] to your RCP endpoint (example: 192.168.1.50:6638) and keep DEVICE as /tmp/ttyOTBR. - [b]USB mode:[/b] clear NETWORK_DEVICE, map your USB device, and set DEVICE to the internal mapped path (default /dev/ttyUSB0).
[b][span style='color: #E80000;']Critical prerequisites:[/span][/b] 1) Enable IPv6 on Unraid host networking and on your LAN (UniFi: enable static IPv6 on the Default network, e.g. fda1:1c14:16ac:0001::/64, with SLAAC + IPv6 DNS). 2) Enable forwarding sysctls (can be automated via User Scripts): [code] sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6=0 sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding=1 sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding=1 sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=2 sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra_rt_info_max_plen=64 [/code] 3) For USB mode, flash your radio with OpenThread RCP firmware (not multiprotocol).
[b]Home Assistant integration:[/b] use OTBR REST URL http://<UNRAID_IP>:8081
[b][span style='color: #E80000;']Important image behavior:[/span][/b] - The image always creates the [i]otbr-ingress-deny-src[/i] ipset on start and tries to destroy it on stop, even when [i]FIREWALL=0[/i]. If the kernel holds a stale reference, the stop/destroy loop can fail and the container crash-loops. - [i]OTBR_REST_PORT[/i] and [i]OTBR_WEB_PORT[/i] are read by this template for documentation only; the upstream image currently hard-codes REST to 8081 and web to 8080. Use the WebUI link accordingly. - [i]NAT64=1[/i] installs iptables NAT64/mangle rules. Disable it if you only need pure Thread/IPv6 and want less netfilter churn.
[b][span style='color: #E80000;']Troubleshooting:[/span][/b] - If the container crash-loops with ipset errors (e.g. [i]Set cannot be destroyed: it is in use by a kernel component[/i]), set [b]FIREWALL=0[/b] and [b]NAT64=0[/b], stop/remove the container, then destroy the stale set from any privileged host-network container: [code]docker run --rm --net=host --privileged alpine sh -c "apk add --no-cache ipset && ipset destroy otbr-ingress-deny-src"[/code] - If REST/Web UI is unreachable, verify the chosen ports are not already in use on the Unraid host. - For commissioning issues, ensure mDNS and IGMP snooping are enabled on your UniFi network.

Config.Name: LAN RCP Endpoint (NETWORK_DEVICE)
Config.Description: Network-based RCP endpoint in IP:PORT format. Default is LAN mode. For USB mode, leave empty.

Config.Name: DEVICE
Config.Description: OTBR radio path. For LAN mode keep /tmp/ttyOTBR. For USB mode set to the internal mapped device path (e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0).

Config.Name: USB Radio Device (optional passthrough)
Config.Description: Optional for USB mode only. Map your physical device (prefer /dev/serial/by-id/...). Leave empty for LAN mode.

Config.Name: Baudrate
Config.Description: Serial baudrate for USB RCP radios.

Config.Name: Flow Control
Config.Description: Hardware flow control for USB radios (1 enabled, 0 disabled).

Config.Name: Backbone Interface
Config.Description: Host network interface used as OTBR backbone (examples: br0, eth0, enp3s0). Must match the interface carrying your LAN IPv6 prefix.

Config.Name: TREL Interface
Config.Description: Optional dedicated TREL interface. If empty, BACKBONE_IF is used.

Config.Name: OTBR WebUI Port
Config.Description: OTBR web interface port. The upstream image hard-codes otbr-web to 8080, so this value must match.

Config.Name: OTBR REST Port
Config.Description: REST API port used by Home Assistant OTBR integration. The image ignores custom values and always listens on 8081 internally.

Config.Name: OTBR Web Interface Bind
Config.Description: Optional bind address for otbr-web (e.g. 0.0.0.0 or ::). Leave empty to use image default.

Config.Name: OTBR Web Enable
Config.Description: Enable the OTBR web UI (1 enabled, 0 disabled).

Config.Name: Enable NAT64
Config.Description: Enable NAT64 translation for IPv4 access from Thread devices.

Config.Name: Enable Firewall
Config.Description: Enable OTBR internal firewall. Disable only for troubleshooting ipset lockups.

Config.Name: Thread 1.4 Beta
Config.Description: Enable Thread 1.4+ beta binaries (set to 1).

Config.Name: Data Storage
Config.Description: Persistent OTBR data storage (datasets/credentials/state).

Config.Name: TUN Adapter
Config.Description: Required for creating wpan/tunnel interfaces.

Config.Name: Local Time
Config.Description: Sync timezone/time from host.

Config.Name: DBus
Config.Description: Optional host D-Bus socket for mDNS/Avahi integration. Leave empty if not needed.

Config.Name: Auto-Flash Firmware
Config.Description: Auto-flash firmware (mainly for HA SkyConnect/Yellow scenarios).

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: OTBR log level (debug,info,notice,warning,error,critical,alert,emergency).
